meta.title: loqu, locut

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: loqu, locut
root.mentor_note: loqu and locut are the same root wearing two different spellings, one for nouns, one for verbs.

attempt.direction_prompt: Struggling for words, or speaking with real skill?

context_sentences: The closing argument was remembered for years, less for its facts than for its sheer eloquence.
Nerves stripped away all his usual eloquence the moment he stepped up to the microphone.
The actor’s soliloquy held the entire theatre silent for nearly four unbroken minutes.
Students are asked to memorise one soliloquy each term, chosen from a different play.
Weekly elocution lessons had smoothed out every trace of his old stammer by the following year.
Her elocution was so precise that radio producers assumed she had trained for broadcasting.

construct_options.text: The skill of clear, expressive public speaking
A speech given by a character alone on stage
The complete inability to speak clearly

context_sentences: His speech collapsed into grandiloquence, ten long words where two plain ones would have done.
The committee preferred a short, direct memo to another round of the chairman’s grandiloquence.

construct_options.text: Speech that is pompous or overly elaborate, meant to impress
Speech that is direct, plain, and easy to follow
A speech delivered by a character completely alone
